title: 7 Herbal Creams to Help Alleviate Gum Swelling

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for gum swelling because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Tannins are good for gum swelling because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain by constricting blood vessels and preventing infection in the affected area.
Polyphenols are good for gum swelling because they have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce swelling and fight the bacteria that cause infections in the gums.
Cichoric acid, commonly known as tea tree oil, is good for gum swelling because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams can be helpful for gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties. When you apply the cream to your gums, it can reduce swelling and pain by blocking the production of chemicals that cause inflammation. This is due to the presence of compounds like alkylamides and glycosides in Echinacea purpurea, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects. By applying the cream to the affected area, you can help to calm the inflammation and reduce the swelling in your gums. The cream can also help to soothe and calm the gums, making it easier to eat and speak comfortably. Echinacea purpurea creams can be a natural and gentle way to manage gum swelling and discomfort. The cream can be applied several times a day to help to reduce the swelling and promote healing.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melaleuca alternifolia herbal cream that helps with gum swelling, you'll need some basic ingredients and equipment. First, you'll need to harvest or purchase high-quality Melaleuca alternifolia leaves, which contain the active ingredient called tea tree oil. You can make tea tree oil by steaming the leaves and then distilling the steam to collect the oil. Next, mix the tea tree oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a 1:10 ratio. This means for every 1 part of tea tree oil, you'll use 10 parts of the carrier oil. After that, add a thickening agent like beeswax or candelilla wax to the mixture and heat it gently until the wax melts. Finally, pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. You'll now have a homemade Melaleuca alternifolia herbal cream that you can apply to swollen gums to help reduce inflammation and pain.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for gum swelling

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anthraquinones are good for gum swelling because they have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain in the gums by blocking the release of chemicals that cause inflammation.
Aloin is good for gum swelling because its anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain, making it a natural remedy for soothing and calming irritated gums.
Tannins are good for gum swelling because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the gums by stopping the growth of bacteria that cause infections.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis cream is a popular remedy for gum swelling due to its anti-inflammatory properties. When you have swollen gums, it's usually because of infection or irritation. The cream contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help reduce swelling and inflammation. These compounds have antioxidant properties that fight free radicals and prevent damage to the gum tissue. The cream also soothes and calms the gum area, reducing redness and pain. Additionally, aloe vera has antibacterial properties that help prevent the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections. By applying aloe barbadensis cream to your gums, you can reduce swelling, ease pain, and promote healthy gum tissue. The cream is also gentle and non-irritating, making it suitable for most people. When used regularly, it can help prevent gum swelling and promote overall gum health.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melaleuca alternifolia herbal cream to help with gum swelling, you'll need a few simple ingredients. First, you'll need to extract the oil from the leaves of the Melaleuca alternifolia plant, also known as tea tree oil. You can do this by steaming the leaves or using a solvent like coconut oil. Once you have the oil, you'll need to mix it with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil to dilute its strong scent and make it safe for skin use. Next, you'll need to add a thickening agent like beeswax or shea butter to create a creamy texture. You can do this by melting the beeswax or shea butter in a double boiler and then mixing it with the oil mixture. Finally, you'll need to pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once it's set, you can use it to help soothe and reduce gum swelling by applying it directly to the affected area with a cotton swab.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for gum swelling

Polyphenols are good for gum swelling because they have strong anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums.
Cineole is good for gum swelling because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight bacteria, making it a natural remedy to soothe inflamed gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams are good for gum swelling because they have anti-inflammatory properties. This means they help reduce swelling and pain in the gums. The active ingredients in these creams, like eucalyptol, work by blocking the release of chemicals that cause swelling. This leads to a decrease in swelling and discomfort.  Eucalyptus globulus creams also have antiseptic properties, which help prevent infection in the gums. This is especially important if you have gum swelling due to an injury or gum disease. The antiseptic properties in these creams help kill bacteria that can cause infection and make the swelling worse. When you apply Eucalyptus globulus creams to your gums, they help reduce redness and swelling. They also have a cooling effect, which can make your gums feel better. The creams are easy to apply and can be used several times a day to help manage gum swelling. The ingredients in Eucalyptus globulus creams are gentle on the gums and can be used by people of all ages. They are also non-irritating and don't have any strong smells or tastes. This makes them a good option for people who are sensitive to certain ingredients or have allergies. Some people use Eucalyptus globulus creams to help manage gum swelling caused by mouth ulcers, gum disease, or tooth extraction. These creams can be used in combination with other treatments, like antibiotics or painkillers, to help manage symptoms and promote healing.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melaleuca alternifolia herbal cream that helps with gum swelling, you will need to extract the essential oil from the leaves of the Melaleuca alternifolia plant, also known as tea tree oil, and then infuse it into a base cream. First, dry the leaves of the plant thoroughly, either by air-drying them or using a food dehydrator on a low temperature setting. Next, use a solvent like ethanol or glycerin to extract the essential oil from the dried leaves. The ratio of solvent to leaves is usually around 1:10, but it may vary depending on the desired strength of the oil. Once the oil is extracted, mix it with a base cream, typically made from ingredients like coconut oil, beeswax, and vitamin E, to create a smooth and non-greasy texture. The base cream should be heated until the beeswax melts, then cooled until it solidifies, allowing the tea tree oil to infuse evenly. Finally, once the cream has solidified, it can be applied directly to the swollen gum area to help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for gum swelling

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis cream is a natural remedy that can help reduce gum swelling. This is because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the affected area. When applied to the gums, the cream can reduce redness and swelling, making it easier to eat and talk. The cream also has antimicrobial properties that help prevent infection and promote healing. By reducing inflammation and preventing infection, Calendula officinalis cream can help gum swelling go down faster. The cream is also gentle and non-irritating, making it suitable for people with sensitive teeth and gums. Additionally, it can help reduce pain and discomfort associated with gum swelling, making it a good option for people who want a natural and gentle remedy.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melaleuca alternifolia herbal cream that helps with gum swelling, you will need a few ingredients and some basic equipment. First, you need to make a Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil infusion by mixing 1 part of dried Melaleuca alternifolia leaves with 2 parts of a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a clean glass jar. Then, you should place the jar in a saucepan of simmering water and let it steep for 2-3 hours. After the infusion is ready, strain it through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container. Next, you need to create a cream base by mixing 1/2 cup of beeswax with 1/2 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring constantly until the beeswax has melted. Once the cream base has cooled slightly, you can add the Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil infusion, a few drops of peppermint essential oil to help with pain and inflammation, and a teaspoon of vitamin E oil to enhance the skin's healing properties. Finally, pour the mixture into small jars or containers and let it cool and solidify before use. This herbal cream can be applied topically to help reduce gum swelling and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for gum swelling

Salvianolic acid is good for gum swelling because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the gums by blocking the production of inflammatory chemicals.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis cream is good for gum swelling because it has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums. The cream contains compounds like triterpenoids and flavonoids, which are known to fight infections and reduce inflammation. When applied to the gums, these compounds can help calm down the swelling and discomfort. Salvia officinalis cream is also rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals. This can help prevent gum disease and promote healthy gum tissue. Additionally, the cream has antimicrobial properties, which can help fight off bacteria and other germs that can cause gum infections and swelling.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams that help with gum swelling, you'll need to combine the healing properties of this plant with a carrier oil and other ingredients. First, start by infusing Melaleuca alternifolia leaves in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil by placing the leaves in a clean glass jar and covering them with the oil. Let this mixture sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the oil extract the plant's goodness. Once the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the leaves. Next, combine the infused oil with a moisturizing ingredient like beeswax or shea butter to create a smooth and creamy texture. Finally, add a few drops of tea tree essential oil, which is also derived from Melaleuca alternifolia and has antibacterial properties that can help reduce gum swelling. Mix everything together until you have a smooth, consistent cream that you can apply directly to swollen gums to soothe and calm them.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams, also known as peppermint creams, can be helpful for gum swelling. The active ingredient in peppermint, menthol, helps to numb the area and reduce pain. This makes it easier to eat and talk without feeling uncomfortable. The cooling sensation from the menthol also helps to reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums. Additionally, peppermint has antibacterial properties that help prevent infection and promote healing. When applied topically, peppermint creams can be absorbed quickly into the skin, providing fast relief from gum swelling and discomfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melaleuca alternifolia herbal cream that helps with gum swelling, you will need a few natural ingredients. First, gather 10 grams of dried and powdered Melaleuca alternifolia leaves, also known as tea tree leaves, which are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties. Next, combine the powdered leaves with 20 grams of beeswax and 40 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. As the beeswax melts, add 10 grams of shea butter and 10 grams of vitamin E oil to the mixture and stir well. Once the mixture has cooled slightly, add 10 milliliters of aloe vera gel and 5 milliliters of apple cider vinegar to help soothe and calm the gums. Finally, pour the mixture into a clean and sterilized container and let it cool completely before using it to apply a thin layer to the affected gum area to reduce swelling and inflammation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia cream, also known as tea tree oil cream, is good for gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties. When your gums get swollen, it can be painful and uncomfortable. The cream works by reducing swelling and easing pain. It contains compounds that stop the growth of bacteria and other germs that can cause gum infections. This helps to prevent further swelling and discomfort. The cream also has antiseptic properties, which help to kill germs and heal wounds quickly. When applied to the affected area, it helps to reduce swelling and ease pain, making it easier to chew and talk. The cream is also easy to apply and can be used as a mouthwash or applied directly to the gums. It is a natural and effective way to treat gum swelling and infections.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams that help with gum swelling, you will need a few simple ingredients and some basic equipment. Start by infusing dried Melaleuca alternifolia leaves in a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil for a few weeks to create a potent extract. This is done by placing the dried leaves in a clean glass jar, covering them with the carrier oil, and letting it sit in a cool, dark place, shaking the jar every few days. After a few weeks, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Then, mix the infused oil with a thickening agent such as beeswax or shea butter, and a preservative like vitamin E oil, to create a smooth and stable cream. Finally, add a few drops of essential oil of Melaleuca alternifolia for its antimicrobial properties, and pour the mixture into a clean glass jar. This herbal cream can be applied directly to the affected area to help reduce gum swelling and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for gum swelling

intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a great solution for gum swelling. One of the key herbs used in these creams is Echinacea purpurea, which is known for its anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, it can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums. Another herb, Aloe barbadensis, is also used due to its soothing and moisturizing properties, which can help calm irritated gums and promote healing.  Gum swelling, also known as gingivitis, can be a sign of an underlying issue with your oral health, such as poor hygiene or a bacterial infection. If left untreated, it can lead to more serious problems like periodontitis, which can cause teeth to fall out. Fixing this issue is crucial because it can affect not only your oral health but also your overall well-being. When you have a healthy mouth, you're less likely to experience problems like bad breath, pain while eating, or difficulty speaking. By using herbal creams containing Echinacea purpurea and Aloe barbadensis, you can start to address the issue of gum swelling and improve your quality of life. Regular use can help prevent further inflammation and promote a healthy, pain-free mouth.  Applying herbal creams can also help you avoid the need for costly dental treatments or antibiotics, which can have unpleasant side effects. By taking proactive steps to address gum swelling, you can regain confidence in your smile and overall health.
supplementary_best: To remedy gum swelling, a combination of Aloe barbadensis, Echinacea purpurea, and Melaleuca alternifolia is effective. Aloe barbadensis reduces inflammation and soothes pain with its anti-inflammatory properties and cooling sensation. Echinacea purpurea boosts the immune system, fighting off infections that cause gum swelling. Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil, has antibacterial properties that kill bacteria causing infection and inflammation. This combination targets gum swelling from multiple angles, reducing inflammation, fighting off infection, and soothing pain.
supplementary_avoid: If you have gum swelling, there are some herbal creams you should avoid. One such herb is 'Capsicum annuum' or Cayenne pepper, which may irritate the gums further and make the swelling worse. Another herb to avoid is 'Sanguinaria canadensis' or Bloodroot, as it can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions. 'Stillingia sylvatica' or Queen's Root is also not recommended, as it can cause skin problems and interact with certain medications. 'Arctium lappa' or Burdock root can cause allergic reactions and skin irritation in some people. 'Glycyrrhiza glabra' or Liquorice root should also be avoided, as it can cause hormonal imbalances and interact with certain medications, which may worsen gum swelling.
supplementary_other_remedies: Saltwater rinse is a common natural remedy for gum swelling. When you swish saltwater in your mouth, the salt helps reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that cause gum swelling. The saltwater also loosens any food particles stuck between your teeth, which can contribute to gum swelling.  Cold compress or an ice pack can also be applied to the swollen area to reduce swelling and ease pain. When you apply cold, the blood vessels constrict, reducing blood flow to the area and the swelling decreases. The cold also numbs the area, making it less painful.  Aloe vera juice has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce gum swelling. When you drink aloe vera juice, the anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation and soothe the gums, making it less swollen. Aloe vera also has antibacterial properties that help kill bacteria that cause gum disease.  Gargling with baking soda and water can also help reduce gum swelling. Baking soda has antibacterial properties that help kill bacteria that cause gum disease. When you gargle with baking soda and water, the baking soda helps neutralize the acid in your mouth, which can contribute to gum swelling.  Some people also use essential oils, like tea tree oil, to help reduce gum swelling. Tea tree oil has antibacterial properties that help kill bacteria that cause gum disease. However, essential oils should be used with caution and only under the guidance of a dentist or a healthcare professional.  Epsom salt can also be used to reduce gum swelling. Epsom salt has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and swelling. When you soak your mouth with Epsom salt water, the anti-inflammatory compounds help soothe the gums and reduce swelling.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have gum swelling, also known as gingivitis, often experience other ailments that can be treated with medicinal herbs. One common condition is bad breath (halitosis), which is caused by bacteria in the mouth. The herb peppermint can help to kill these bacteria and reduce bad breath.  Another issue is tooth decay (cavities), which can be treated with the herb clove. Clove has antibacterial properties that help to kill the bacteria that cause tooth decay.  People with gum swelling may also experience tooth sensitivity (hypersensitivity), which can be treated with the herb licorice root. Licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums.  Some people with gum swelling may also experience bleeding gums, which can be treated with the herb aloe vera. Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums.  In addition, people with gum swelling may experience dry mouth (xerostomia), which can be treated with the herb slippery elm. Slippery elm helps to stimulate saliva production, which can help to prevent tooth decay and bad breath.  Finally, people with gum swelling may experience mouth ulcers (aphthous ulcers), which can be treated with the herb myrrh. Myrrh has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the mouth.
